When there are disagreements between people, those who are most willing to forgive or forget, and most ready to yield for the sake of peace, should be considered the wisest and best. In the methods that Samson used, we must see God’s power as providing them and making them successful, to mortify pride and punish the wickedness of the Philistines. The Philistines threatened Samson's wife that they would burn her and her father's house. She, to save herself and please her countrymen, betrayed her husband; and the very thing that she feared, and by sin sought to avoid, came upon her! She, and her father's house, were burnt with fire, and by her countrymen, whom she thought to please by the wrong she did to her husband. The trouble we try to escape by any unlawful practices, we often bring down on our own heads.

Sin discourages people; it hides from their eyes things that belong to their peace. The Israelites blamed Samson for what he had done against the Philistines, as if he had harmed them greatly. Thus our Lord Jesus did many good works, and for those the Jews were ready to stone him. When the Spirit of the Lord came upon Samson, his cords were loosed: where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty, and those are free indeed who are set free in this way. Thus Christ triumphed over the powers of darkness that shouted against him, as if they had him in their power. Samson caused great destruction among the Philistines. To take the bone of an ass for this, was to do wonders by foolish things of the world, so that the excellence of the power might be of God, not of man. This victory was not in the weapon, nor in the arm; but it was in the Spirit of God, which moved the weapon by the arm. We can do all things through Him who strengthens us. Do you see a poor Christian, who is enabled to overcome a temptation by weak, feeble counsel? There is the Philistine vanquished by a sorry jawbone.

The men of Judah paid so little attention to their deliverer that he was ready to perish for lack of water. Thus are the greatest slights often put upon those who do the greatest services. Samson prayed to God in this distress. Those who forget to attend God with praise, may be compelled to attend him with prayer. Past experiences of God’s power and goodness are excellent pleas in prayer for further mercy. He pleads his being exposed to God's enemies; our best pleas are taken from God's glory. The Lord sent him timely relief. The place of this action was called Lehi, from the jawbone. And in the place thus called, God caused a fountain suddenly and seasonably to open close by Samson. We should be more thankful for the mercy of water if we considered how ill we can spare it. Israel submitted to him whom they had betrayed. God was with him; henceforth they were directed by him as their judge.
